items.name: Efficient-Heart-Disease-Prediction-project-using-Machine-Learning
items.description: Explainable Ensemble Machine Learning Framework for Heart Disease Prediction using Random Forest, XGBoost, SHAP, and Streamlit. This system predicts heart disease risk based on clinical parameters and provides interpretable AI-driven insights for healthcare decision support.

items.name: Symptom-Based-Tuberculosis-Detection-using-Machine-Learning
items.description: Developed an explainable machine learning framework for early tuberculosis risk prediction using clinical symptoms. Compared Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and XGBoost models, achieving 99.31% accuracy and 0.997 ROC-AUC. Applied SHAP explainability to identify clinically significant symptoms contributing to TB prediction.

items.name: Blockchain-Enabled-Peer-to-Peer-Energy-Trading-with-Machine-Learning
items.description: This project proposes an intelligent peer-to-peer energy trading framework integrating IEEE distribution networks, machine learning, and blockchain technology. Machine learning predicts energy demand and generation, while blockchain enables secure, transparent, and decentralized energy transactions.

items.name: Predicting-Market-Regimes-and-Volatility-Using-HMM-and-Hybrid-Machine-LearningTechniques
items.description: A hybrid HMM-LSTM framework that predicts market regimes and forecasts volatility. It pairs a Gaussian HMM for latent state detection with a 2-layer LSTM to eliminate state blindness. Tested on 2000–2026 data, it achieves 84% regime accuracy and a 53% reduction in RMSE over standard baselines

items.name: pose-based-human-activity-recognition
items.description: A modular Human Activity Recognition framework using 2D pose estimation. Includes skeleton normalization, feature engineering, multi-scale temporal windowing, and subject-independent evaluation with support for traditional machine learning and deep learning models.

By analyzing historical records of over 20,000 Sri Lankan offshore fishing trips (2022–2025) across 30+ operational variables.

items.name: healthcare-risk-prediction-ensemble-learning
items.description: This project develops an end-to-end machine learning framework for predicting heart attack risk using healthcare survey data. The solution goes beyond traditional classification by implementing a stacked ensemble architecture that combines multiple machine learning algorithms to improve predictive performance, robustness, and generalization.

items.name: Path-Loss-Prediction-Using-Machine-Learning
items.description: Optimized stacking ensemble framework for path loss prediction in 5G mmWave wireless communication systems using Random Forest, SVR, and Gradient Boosting.

items.name: MicroTorch
items.description: MicroTorch is an educational deep learning framework designed to expose the internals of modern machine learning libraries by implementing every core component from first principles. Instead of relying on PyTorch or TensorFlow, MicroTorch builds automatic differentiation, backpropagation, neural network layers, and optimization directly in Python.

items.name: ASO_SOD1-SMN2_Prediction

items.name: CHHO-GSA-for-Voice-Based-Parkinson-s-Disease-Detection
items.description: CHHO-GSA is a hybrid feature selection framework for early Parkinson’s Disease detection using voice data. It combines Chaotic Harris Hawks Optimization (CHHO) and the Gravitational Search Algorithm (GSA) to select optimal acoustic features and improve classification performance using machine learning models.

items.name: TCAD-Inspired-FinFET-Modeling-Performance-Prediction-and-Power-Optimization-Using-Machine-Learning
items.description: TCAD-inspired FinFET modeling and optimization framework using Machine Learning. Predicts drain current (Id), delay, and power consumption with Random Forest Regression, provides interactive design exploration, and performs PAL-based optimization for low-power device configurations.
